expected.equipment: list equipment where status=Quarantined and expires_on is past due

actual.equipment: Query equipment items where status = 'Quarantined' AND expires_on < current_date (i.e., inspection/calibration expiry is in the past). Return fields: equipment_id, name, generic_name, manufacturer, model, serial_number, verification_type, expires_on, status, area_id (with area name), job_id. Sort by expires_on ascending and include total count and pagination support.

details.expected: list equipment where status=Quarantined and expires_on is past due

Sort by expires_on ascending and include total count and pagination support.

expected.job: create job: job_name=PA Install Demo, client_name=Demo Facilities, equipment_requests=[cordless drill, bandsaw]

actual.job: Create a new job with job_name 'PA Install Demo', client_name 'Demo Facilities', status 'Pending', site 'Demo Facilities'; generate job_id automatically and include equipment_requests array: [{name: 'Cordless Drill', generic_name: 'DRILL', quantity: 1}, {name: 'Bandsaw', generic_name: 'BANDSAW', quantity: 1}].
actual.equipment: Check inventory for an available Cordless Drill (generic_name DRILL) and Bandsaw (generic_name BANDSAW); if available allocate one of each to the newly created job (set their status to 'Allocated' and set job_id to the job_id generated). If not available, create placeholder equipment request records linked to the job indicating required quantity.

If not available, create placeholder equipment request records linked to the job indicating required quantity.

expected.import: import equipment from Excel file in shared folder

actual.import: Locate the Excel file in the shared folder (detect latest .xlsx/.xls in shared/), then import it as entityType 'equipment' using automatic field mapping and validation; generate missing equipment_id values, validate required fields, and run with dryRun=false to perform the import. On import failure, produce a report of invalid rows and errors.
actual.equipment: Create or update equipment records using imported rows: set fields (equipment_id, name, manufacturer, model, serial_number, status, area_id, job_id, verification_type, expires_on, generic_name). For rows matching existing equipment_id or serial_number, update the existing record; otherwise create new equipment items and link to areas/jobs where provided.

details.expected: import equipment from Excel file in shared folder
details.actual: Locate the Excel file in the shared folder (detect latest .xlsx/.xls in shared/), then import it as entityType 'equipment' using automatic field mapping and validation; generate missing equipment_id values, validate required fields, and run with dryRun=false to perform the import. On import failure, produce a report of invalid rows and errors.

Create or update equipment records using imported rows: set fields (equipment_id, name, manufacturer, model, serial_number, status, area_id, job_id, verification_type, expires_on, generic_name). For rows matching existing equipment_id or serial_number, update the existing record; otherwise create new equipment items and link to areas/jobs where provided.

actual.job: Locate the job record with job_name or site 'Smith Construction' (ensure correct job_id and that job is Active or Pending), add CRL0042 to the job's equipment_requests or assigned equipment, and return the job_id for equipment assignment.
actual.area: Identify the current Warehouse area containing CRL0042 to verify source, locate or create the area record for 'Smith Construction' with location_type 'Site' and return its area_id for the equipment update; if creating, populate name and site details.

Locate the job record with job_name or site 'Smith Construction' (ensure correct job_id and that job is Active or Pending), add CRL0042 to the job's equipment_requests or assigned equipment, and return the job_id for equipment assignment.

expected.wipe-data: clear all equipment data from database

actual.wipe-data: Require explicit confirmation from the user; once confirmed, delete all database records (equipment, materials, areas, jobs), reset primary key/ID sequences, clear related audit logs and caches, and produce a summary report of deleted counts and status.
actual.equipment: Backup current equipment dataset, list all equipment items slated for deletion, then remove all equipment records and verify no equipment entries remain; return a post-wipe verification summary.

details.expected: clear all equipment data from database
details.actual: Require explicit confirmation from the user; once confirmed, delete all database records (equipment, materials, areas, jobs), reset primary key/ID sequences, clear related audit logs and caches, and produce a summary report of deleted counts and status.
Backup current equipment dataset, list all equipment items slated for deletion, then remove all equipment records and verify no equipment entries remain; return a post-wipe verification summary.

expected.show-help: display help information about available commands

actual.equipment: Manage equipment and assets: create/read/update/delete equipment items; search and filter by equipment_id, name, manufacturer, model, serial_number, status, area_id, job_id, verification_type, expires_on, generic_name; allocate/return/change status and view equipment assignments.
actual.job: Manage jobs/projects/work orders: create/read/update/delete jobs with job_id, job_name, client_name, status, site, requested_by, date_from/date_to, equipment_requests, materials; assign equipment and materials to jobs and update job status.
actual.area: Manage storage areas and locations: create/read/update/delete areas with area_id, name, location_name, location_type (Warehouse/Van/Site/Storage); assign and view inventory by area and track locations of assets/materials.
actual.material: Manage consumable materials and supplies: create/read/update/delete materials with material_id, name, quantity, unit, reorder_level, job_id, area_id; adjust quantities, track usage, and report on low-stock or reorder needs.
actual.import: Import bulk data from CSV or Excel into equipment, materials, or areas: provide filePath and entityType (equipment/material/area), optional dryRun for validation; perform automatic field mapping, validation, and ID generation for bulk create/update.
actual.wipe-data: Administrative data wipe/reset: clear database data for inventory, jobs, areas, and materials; requires explicit confirmation before execution and should be used only when user explicitly requests a full reset.
actual.show-help: Display help and guidance about available commands, field definitions, usage examples, and recommended workflows for using the system.

details.expected: display help information about available commands
details.actual: Display help and guidance about available commands, field definitions, usage examples, and recommended workflows for using the system.

Manage equipment and assets: create/read/update/delete equipment items; search and filter by equipment_id, name, manufacturer, model, serial_number, status, area_id, job_id, verification_type, expires_on, generic_name; allocate/return/change status and view equipment assignments.

Manage jobs/projects/work orders: create/read/update/delete jobs with job_id, job_name, client_name, status, site, requested_by, date_from/date_to, equipment_requests, materials; assign equipment and materials to jobs and update job status.

Manage storage areas and locations: create/read/update/delete areas with area_id, name, location_name, location_type (Warehouse/Van/Site/Storage); assign and view inventory by area and track locations of assets/materials.

Manage consumable materials and supplies: create/read/update/delete materials with material_id, name, quantity, unit, reorder_level, job_id, area_id; adjust quantities, track usage, and report on low-stock or reorder needs.

Import bulk data from CSV or Excel into equipment, materials, or areas: provide filePath and entityType (equipment/material/area), optional dryRun for validation; perform automatic field mapping, validation, and ID generation for bulk create/update.

Administrative data wipe/reset: clear database data for inventory, jobs, areas, and materials; requires explicit confirmation before execution and should be used only when user explicitly requests a full reset.

actual.material: Manage consumables and supplies: CRUD materials with fields like material_id, name, quantity, unit, reorder_level, and associations to job_id and area_id to track usage and stock levels.
actual.import: Import data in bulk from CSV/Excel for equipment, materials, or areas; automatic field mapping, validation, ID generation, and optional dry-run to verify changes before applying.
actual.wipe-data: Administrative operation to clear the database data; a dangerous action that requires explicit confirmation before execution.
